items.q: Which mood follows 'quiero que' (I want that)?
items.why: Students use indicative after que. Expressions of wish, emotion, doubt, or impersonal expressions trigger SUBJUNCTIVE: 'Quiero que VENGAS' (not 'que vienes'). Different subjects require que + subjunctive.

A notorious faux ami that causes real confusion. 'Estoy embarazada' = 'I am pregnant'. For 'embarrassed' use 'avergonzado/a'. Native speakers laugh at this one - learners avoid by association with shame.

items.q: How is 'll' typically pronounced in Spanish?
items.why: Students pronounce 'll' as English double L. In most Spanish dialects, ll is a YOD sound (like English y) or a 'zh' sound (Argentina). Never pronounced as a doubled English L.

items.q: When did the Civil War end?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: 1939. “1936” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year was Franco exhumed from the Valley of the Fallen?
items.why: Correct answer: 2019. “1975” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many years did Franco's dictatorship last?
items.why: Exact answer: 36. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
